---
title: "plotlycsv Integration Test"
subtitle: "Verifying global and per-plot CSV export decorators"
author: "plotlycsv test suite"
date: today
format:
  html:
    embed-resources: true
    toc: true
    toc-depth: 2
    theme: flatly
execute:
  echo: true
  warning: false
  message: false
---

```{r setup}
#| include: false

# Load the package under test.
# When run via the testthat suite, the package root is passed in via the
# PLOTLYCSV_PKG_PATH environment variable so that pkgload::load_all() can
# find it regardless of the working directory Quarto uses.
pkg_path <- Sys.getenv("PLOTLYCSV_PKG_PATH", unset = NA)
if (!is.na(pkg_path) && nzchar(pkg_path) && file.exists(file.path(pkg_path, "R"))) {
  pkgload::load_all(pkg_path, quiet = TRUE)
} else {
  library(plotlycsv)
}

library(plotly)
library(ggplot2)
```

## Global Decorator

The global decorator (`use_plotly_csv()`) is called **once** in a setup
chunk and automatically attaches a "Download CSV" modebar button to
**every** Plotly widget on the page.  No per-plot code is required.

```{r global-decorator}
# Call use_plotly_csv() once — all plots below will pick up the filename
# automatically from their chunk label (e.g. "global-plot-1.csv").
use_plotly_csv()
```

### Plot 1 — `plot_ly()` scatter (decorated by global hook)

```{r global-plot-1}
plot_ly(
  mtcars,
  x     = ~wt,
  y     = ~mpg,
  color = ~factor(cyl),
  type  = "scatter",
  mode  = "markers"
) |>
  layout(
    title  = "Weight vs MPG (global decorator)",
    xaxis  = list(title = "Weight (1000 lbs)"),
    yaxis  = list(title = "Miles per Gallon")
  )
```

### Plot 2 — `ggplotly()` bar chart (decorated by global hook)

```{r}
#| label: fig-global-plot-2
#| fig-cap: "Average MPG by Cylinder Count"
p <- ggplot(
  aggregate(mpg ~ cyl, data = mtcars, FUN = mean),
  aes(x = factor(cyl), y = mpg, fill = factor(cyl))
) +
  geom_col(show.legend = FALSE) +
  labs(
    title = "Average MPG by Cylinder Count",
    x     = "Cylinders",
    y     = "Mean MPG"
  )

ggplotly(p)
```

---

## Per-Plot Decorator

The per-plot decorator (`add_plotly_export_csv()`) is piped directly onto
an individual widget. It allows a **custom filename** and fine-grained
control over what is exported.

### Plot 3 — `plot_ly()` with per-plot decorator

```{r per-plot-1}
plot_ly(
  mtcars,
  x    = ~hp,
  y    = ~qsec,
  type = "scatter",
  mode = "markers",
  marker = list(color = "#e15759", size = 8)
) |>
  layout(
    title = "Horsepower vs Quarter-Mile Time (per-plot decorator)",
    xaxis = list(title = "Horsepower"),
    yaxis = list(title = "Quarter-Mile Time (s)")
  ) |>
  add_plotly_export_csv(filename = "per_plot_hp_qsec.csv")
```

### Plot 4 — `ggplotly()` with per-plot decorator and `selected_only`

```{r per-plot-2}
p2 <- ggplot(mtcars, aes(disp, drat, colour = factor(am))) +
  geom_point(size = 3) +
  labs(
    title  = "Displacement vs Rear-Axle Ratio",
    colour = "Transmission (0 = auto)"
  )

ggplotly(p2) |>
  add_plotly_export_csv(
    filename      = "per_plot_disp_drat.csv",
    selected_only = FALSE,   # export all points regardless of selection state
    include_text  = TRUE
  )
```

---

## Verification markers

The section below emits sentinel strings into the HTML that the automated
`testthat` integration test uses to confirm the decorators were applied.

```{r verification-markers}
#| results: asis
#| echo: false

cat("<!-- PLOTLYCSV_GLOBAL_OK: auto-filename -->
")
cat("<!-- PLOTLYCSV_PER_PLOT_OK: per_plot_hp_qsec.csv -->
")
cat("<!-- PLOTLYCSV_PER_PLOT_2_OK: per_plot_disp_drat.csv -->
")
```
